meta.title: The Autopoietic Extrusion Engine: Validating Jules for Continuous Ideation
meta.short_tagline: A comprehensively engineered middleware blueprint for wrapping Google's Jules into a zero-HITL, context-rot-resistant software generation engine.

overview.title: Heteronomously Regulated Autopoiesis
overview.intro: Google's Jules is a highly capable execution substrate for asynchronous coding tasks, but treating it as a fully autopoietic entity that can self-organize from abstract research prose to a validated codebase leads to catastrophic semantic drift. This manifesto proves that autonomous fidelity requires an external orchestration wrapper consisting of semantic state-machine gates, AST-based hallucination detection, and saga-style transactional rollbacks.

summary_card.label: Architectural Truth
summary_card.title: Jules is the Substrate, Not the Orchestrator
summary_card.body: Without a mandatory 'architectural checkpoint' between ideation and implementation, agents produce 'Interpretive Fracture'. Meaningful zero-HITL autonomous development relies entirely on external mereological routing—injecting semantic constraints back into the agent's context window at every software layer to prevent the model from forgetting the core research thesis amidst boilerplate generation.

key_concepts.title: Pillars of the Autonomous Extrusion Pipeline

cards.title: The Petzold Sequence
cards.body: An architectural design pattern enforcing a mandatory multi-phase state machine (THINK -> DAG -> CODE -> REVIEW). No phase may begin until the previous phase's output passes a deterministic structural guard, preventing the agent from hallucinating downstream logic based on unresolved architectural planning.

cards.title: Draft-Conditioned Constrained Decoding (DCCD)
cards.body: A training-free inference algorithm that decouples semantic planning from structural JSON schema enforcement. It allows the model to generate a free-form semantic draft before regenerating under hard schema constraints, virtually eliminating the 'projection tax' of standard constrained decoding.

cards.title: Semantic Saponification & ContextLock
cards.body: As context token depth increases, the core research thesis dissolves into surrounding code boilerplate—a phenomenon termed 'Context Rot'. ContextLock utilizes a Semantic Anchor Vector (SAV) that is structurally re-injected at every phase boundary to mathematically anchor generation back to the original text.

cards.title: AST-Based KCH Detection
cards.body: Knowledge Conflicting Hallucinations (KCH)—where the agent calls non-existent APIs—are structurally remediated by parsing the generated code into an Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) and non-probabilistically cross-referencing nodes against introspected knowledge bases of installed open-source libraries.

key_concepts.intro: These critical conceptual frameworks bridge the gap between Jules's raw capabilities and the strict requirements of continuous epistemic ideation.

workflow.title: The Petzold Sequence State Machine

steps.label: Phase 1: THINK (Epistemic Extraction)
steps.summary: The agent reads the source research document and outputs a structured Product Requirements Prompt (PRP) alongside a Semantic Anchor Vector (SAV).
bullets: Ingests raw documents up to 50K tokens without code writing.
Validates output against a strict DCCD-powered JSON schema.
Triggers 'Epistemic Escrow' if the source text is scientifically incoherent.

steps.label: Phase 2: DAG (Architectural Resolution)
steps.summary: The agent leverages the PRP and SAV to construct a topologically sorted Directed Acyclic Graph representing the C4 software architecture.
bullets: Ensures zero circular dependencies.
RAG-validates all component nodes against real package registries (PyPI, npm).
Creates explicit boundaries for subsequent layer extrusion.

steps.label: Phase 3: CODE (Extrusion & AST Validation)
steps.summary: Layer-by-layer extrusion (DB -> Backend -> Frontend -> Tests), executed as completely independent Jules VM tasks.
bullets: Re-injects the SAV at each layer boundary to prevent context rot.
Executes AST validation to correct Knowledge Conflicting Hallucinations.
Initiates 'Saga-Style' file-level transactional rollbacks upon test failure.

steps.label: Phase 4: REVIEW (Automated Alignment Audit)
steps.summary: Analyzes the resulting repository for structural drift and generates the final Defect Remediation Deficit ledger.
bullets: Computes SAV divergence score against the generated codebase.
Emits a comprehensive PR containing all telemetry and code.
Identifies residual epistemic boundaries for human evaluation.

workflow.intro: The end-to-end orchestration flow necessary for transforming unstructured research into AST-validated architecture with zero human-in-the-loop intervention.

checklist.title: Middleware Implementation Checklist

items.label: Trigger & Ingestion Setup
bullets: Deploy a GitHub Actions Webhook with MIME-type detection.
Integrate the Jules API (POST /tasks) with pre-configured VM Snapshot boots.
Configure Epistemic Escrow logic for gracefully rejecting nonsensical inputs.

items.label: Semantic Anchoring Middleware
bullets: Implement a DCCD Schema Guard to enforce the PRP_v1 standard.
Configure YAML extraction logic for the Semantic Anchor Vector (SAV).
Embed JIT-retrieval context-injection for the CODE phase prompts.

items.label: Validation & Rollback Mechanisms
bullets: Set up AST-KCH detector via Python's inspect module for dynamic library validation.
Define PIPELINE_LAYERS in the Saga Orchestrator to dictate commit precedence (e.g., DB before Backend).
Link testing suites (pytest/npm) to Saga's automated partial rollback limits (max 3 retries).

checklist.intro: Actionable steps to bind Jules to the custom Python orchestration middleware required for the Autopoietic Extrusion Engine.

metrics.intro: The mathematical realities governing autonomous generation capabilities and expected token expenditures.

items.name: Defect Remediation Deficit (DRD)
items.description: The cumulative token burn and clock time required to remediate autonomous failures relative to first-pass generation.
signals: Target bounds: 1.8x - 2.4x the clean generation cost.
Influenced heavily by semantic saponification retry cycles.
Must be tracked to prevent unbounded infinite loop generation.

items.name: KCH Detection Metrics
items.description: The effectiveness of the Abstract Syntax Tree validation at intercepting hallucinated software dependencies.
signals: Auto-correction accuracy target: ~77% without manual intervention.

items.name: Context Retention Fidelity
items.description: Evaluation of model recall and constraint obedience as token length expands toward 50K limits.
signals: Target F1 score > 0.65 on research thesis recall.

metrics.title: Telemetry & Acceptability Thresholds

personas.intro: The logical multi-agent topology functioning inside the pipeline's execution gates.

cells: Semantic Anchor Vector (SAV) & Product Requirements Prompt

personas.title: The Orchestrator Triad

failure_modes.name: Epistemic Escrow Triggering
failure_modes.description: The research document lacks computational validity or coherent reasoning (e.g. 'The aerodynamics of cheese in a vacuum').
mitigations: Halt the pipeline at the THINK phase.
Compute entity density and implementability score.
Generate an escrow report via GitHub Issue instead of writing hallucinated code.

failure_modes.name: Autophagic Memory Traps
failure_modes.description: The agent attempts to write the full-stack architecture in one pass, leading it to rewrite its own earlier structural assumptions mid-generation.
mitigations: Pass previous layers as read-only context to subsequent tasks.

failure_modes.name: Semantic Saponification
failure_modes.description: Context degradation causing the agent's structural output to drift away from the core thesis of the ingested research as boilerplate scales.
mitigations: Append the highly compressed SAV (Semantic Anchor Vector) to both the head and tail of every executed prompt.

resilience.intro: Known systemic vulnerabilities resulting from LLM context boundaries, and their respective architectural countermeasures.
resilience.title: Defect Matrices and Rollbacks
